title: asclepias minor
intro: Asclepias minor, also known as Antelope Horn or Dwarf Milkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of cardenolides and other compounds. The health benefits associated with Asclepias minor include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antispasmodic agent. In herbal preparations, Asclepias minor is often dried and used as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
